message.content: Add comprehensive JSDoc comments to every exported function in apps/api/src/service.ts and apps/api/src/agentClient.ts. Document purpose, parameters, return values, thrown errors, and the freshness and fallback branching behavior. Match the doc comment style already used in packages/data/src/sync.ts. addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)
- oh-my-claudecode:analyst: Pre-planning consultant for requirements analysis (Opus) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)
- oh-my-claudecode:architect: Strategic Architecture & Debugging Advisor (Opus, READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)

- oh-my-claudecode:code-reviewer: Expert code review specialist with severity-rated feedback, logic defect detection, SOLID principle checks, style, performance, and quality strategy (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)

- oh-my-claudecode:code-simplifier: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise. (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:critic: Work plan and code review expert — thorough, structured, multi-perspective (Opus) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)
- oh-my-claudecode:debugger: Root-cause analysis, regression isolation, stack trace analysis, build/compilation error resolution (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:designer: UI/UX Designer-Developer for stunning interfaces (Sonnet) (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:document-specialist: External Documentation & Reference Specialist (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)
- oh-my-claudecode:executor: Focused task executor for implementation work (Sonnet) (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:explore: Codebase search specialist for finding files and code patterns (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)
- oh-my-claudecode:git-master: Git expert for atomic commits, rebasing, and history management with style detection (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:planner: Strategic planning consultant with interview workflow (Opus) (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:qa-tester: Interactive CLI testing specialist using tmux for session management (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:scientist: Data analysis and research execution specialist (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)
- oh-my-claudecode:security-reviewer: Security vulnerability detection specialist (OWASP Top 10, secrets, unsafe patterns) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)
- oh-my-claudecode:test-engineer: Test strategy, integration/e2e coverage, flaky test hardening, TDD workflows (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:tracer: Evidence-driven causal tracing with competing hypotheses, evidence for/against, uncertainty tracking, and next-probe recommendations (Tools: All tools)
- oh-my-claudecode:verifier: Verification strategy, evidence-based completion checks, test adequacy (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit)
- oh-my-claudecode:writer: Technical documentation writer for README, API docs, and comments (Haiku) (Tools: All tools)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- omc-reference: OMC agent catalog, available tools, team pipeline routing, commit protocol, and skills registry. Auto-loads when delegating to agents, using OMC tools, orchestrating teams, making commits, or invoking skills.
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- oh-my-claudecode:ai-slop-cleaner: Clean AI-generated code slop with a regression-safe, deletion-first workflow and optional reviewer-only mode
- oh-my-claudecode:ask: Process-first advisor routing for Claude, Codex, Gemini, Grok, or Cursor via `omc ask`, with artifact capture and no raw CLI assembly
- oh-my-claudecode:autopilot: Full autonomous execution from idea to working code
- oh-my-claudecode:autoresearch: Stateful single-mission improvement loop with strict evaluator contract, markdown decision logs, and max-runtime stop behavior
- oh-my-claudecode:cancel: Cancel any active OMC mode (autopilot, ralph, ultrawork, ultraqa, swarm, ultrapilot, pipeline, team)
- oh-my-claudecode:ccg: Claude-Codex-Gemini tri-model orchestration via /ask codex + /ask gemini, then Claude synthesizes results
- oh-my-claudecode:configure-notifications: Configure notification integrations (Telegram, Discord, Slack) via natural language
- oh-my-claudecode:debug: Diagnose the current OMC session or repo state using logs, traces, state, and focused reproduction
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-dive: 2-stage pipeline: trace (causal investigation) -> deep-interview (requirements crystallization) with 3-point injection
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-interview: Socratic deep interview with mathematical ambiguity gating before explicit execution approval
- oh-my-claudecode:deepinit: Deep codebase initialization with hierarchical AGENTS.md documentation
- oh-my-claudecode:external-context: Invoke parallel document-specialist agents for external web searches and documentation lookup
- oh-my-claudecode:hud: Configure HUD display options (layout, presets, display elements)
- oh-my-claudecode:learner: Extract a learned skill from the current conversation
- oh-my-claudecode:local-build-reminder: Remind the user to rebuild OMC after editing TypeScript when running from a local fork. Triggered automatically by the AI whenever it notices it (or the user) just changed a src/**/*.ts file in an OMC dev install.
- oh-my-claudecode:mcp-setup: Configure popular MCP servers for enhanced agent capabilities
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-doctor: Diagnose and fix oh-my-claudecode installation issues
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-reference: OMC agent catalog, available tools, team pipeline routing, commit protocol, and skills registry. Auto-loads when delegating to agents, using OMC tools, orchestrating teams, making commits, or invoking skills.
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-setup: Install or refresh oh-my-claudecode for plugin, npm, and local-dev setups from the canonical setup flow
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-teams: CLI-team runtime for claude, codex, or gemini workers in tmux panes when you need process-based parallel execution
- oh-my-claudecode:plan: Strategic planning with optional interview workflow
- oh-my-claudecode:project-session-manager: Worktree-first dev environment manager for issues, PRs, and features with optional tmux sessions
- oh-my-claudecode:ralph: Self-referential loop until task completion with configurable verification reviewer
- oh-my-claudecode:ralplan: Consensus planning entrypoint that auto-gates vague ralph/autopilot/team requests before execution
- oh-my-claudecode:release: Generic release assistant — analyzes repo release rules, caches them in .omc/RELEASE_RULE.md, then guides the release
- oh-my-claudecode:remember: Review reusable project knowledge and decide what belongs in project memory, notepad, or durable docs
- oh-my-claudecode:sciomc: Orchestrate parallel scientist agents for comprehensive analysis with AUTO mode
- oh-my-claudecode:self-improve: Autonomous evolutionary code improvement engine with tournament selection
- oh-my-claudecode:setup: Use first for install/update routing — sends setup, doctor, or MCP requests to the correct OMC setup flow
- oh-my-claudecode:skill: Manage local skills - list, add, remove, search, edit, setup wizard
- oh-my-claudecode:skillify: Turn a repeatable workflow from the current session into a reusable OMC skill draft
- oh-my-claudecode:team: N coordinated agents on shared task list using Claude Code native teams
- oh-my-claudecode:trace: Evidence-driven tracing lane that orchestrates competing tracer hypotheses in Claude built-in team mode
- oh-my-claudecode:ultragoal: Durable multi-goal workflow that persists plan/ledger artifacts under .omc/ultragoal and prints Claude /goal handoff text for the active session
- oh-my-claudecode:ultraqa: QA cycling workflow - test, verify, fix, repeat until goal met
- oh-my-claudecode:ultrawork: Parallel execution engine for high-throughput task completion
- oh-my-claudecode:verify: Verify that a change really works before you claim completion
- oh-my-claudecode:visual-verdict: Structured visual QA verdict for screenshot-to-reference comparisons
- oh-my-claudecode:wiki: LLM Wiki — persistent markdown knowledge base that compounds across sessions (Karpathy model)
- oh-my-claudecode:writer-memory: Agentic memory system for writers - track characters, relationships, scenes, and themes
- telegram:access: Manage Telegram channel access — approve pairings, edit allowlists, set DM/group policy. Use when the user asks to pair, approve someone, check who's allowed, or change policy for the Telegram channel.
- telegram:configure: Set up the Telegram channel — save the bot token and review access policy. Use when the user pastes a Telegram bot token, asks to configure Telegram, asks "how do I set this up" or "who can reach me," or wants to check channel status.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Now I understand the code and the style. The `sync.ts` reference uses concise `/** */` JSDoc prose. Let me add comprehensive JSDoc in that style, with `@param`/`@returns`/`@throws` and emphasis on the freshness/fallback branching.

Let me start with `service.ts`:

input.new_string:
/**
 * Produces lottery number recommendations for the requested draw, enriched with
 * LLM-authored explanations when the agent is reachable.
 *
 * Freshness branching: unless `skipLiveSync` is set, the latest draws are pulled
 * from the provider via {@link syncDrawsBeforeServing} and the outcome is
 * classified into `freshness`. If the sync throws, freshness falls back to
 * `last-good` — the stored `latestDrawNo` is reported alongside a `syncErrorKind`
 * derived from the error (the {@link DrawBackfillError} code, the error name, or
 * `"UnknownError"`) instead of propagating the failure. When `skipLiveSync` is
 * set, no provider call is made and freshness is reported as `skipped` against
 * the locally stored latest draw.
 *
 * Fallback branching: numbers are always generated deterministically and the
 * response starts with `fallbackUsed: true`. If at least one combination is
 * produced, {@link explainWithAgent} is called; its `fallbackUsed` flag and
 * per-combination explanations are merged in, so a reachable, valid agent
 * downgrades `fallbackUsed` to `false` while an unavailable or invalid agent
 * leaves the deterministic explanations in place.
 *
 * @param body - Unvalidated request payload, parsed by `recommendationRequestSchema`.
 * @param opts - Optional overrides: `agentUrl` for the explanation service,
 *   `skipLiveSync` to bypass the provider sync, and `provider` to inject a
 *   `LotteryResultProvider` (defaults to {@link DhlotteryJsonProvider}).
 * @returns The recommendation response including numbers, freshness, feasibility,
 *   disclaimers, and (when available) agent explanations.
 * @throws {ZodError} If `body` fails `recommendationRequestSchema` validation.
 * @throws {Error} With `status: 400` if `targetDrawNo` is not later than the
 *   latest synced draw.
 */
export async function createRecommendations(
  body: unknown,
  opts: { agentUrl?: string; skipLiveSync?: boolean; provider?: LotteryResultProvider } = {},
): Promise<RecommendationResponse> {

/**
 * Validates that each supplied combination is six ascending numbers.
 *
 * @param body - Payload shaped as `{ combinations?: number[][] }`; a missing
 *   `combinations` is treated as an empty list (which is vacuously `valid`).
 * @returns An object with `valid` (true only if every combination passes) and
 *   `results`, the per-combination `{ numbers, valid }` verdicts.

/**
 * Ranks a previously saved recommendation against an actual draw result and
 * persists the check.
 *
 * Target-draw branching: when `drawNo` is supplied it is used directly;
 * otherwise the recommendation's own `targetDrawNo` is ranked. In the latter
 * case, if that draw has not yet occurred (latest stored draw is earlier than
 * the target), the request is rejected rather than silently clamped to an
 * earlier result, so no fake check is persisted.
 *
 * @param id - Identifier of the saved recommendation.
 * @param drawNo - Optional explicit draw number to rank against; defaults to the
 *   saved recommendation's `targetDrawNo`.
 * @returns The match outcome (`matchedNumbers`, `bonusMatched`, `rank`) along
 *   with `recommendationId`, `drawNo`, and the persisted `checkId`.
 * @throws {Error} With `status: 404` if the saved recommendation or the draw
 *   result is not found.
 * @throws {Error} With `status: 409` if the recommendation's target draw has not
 *   been drawn yet (only when `drawNo` is omitted).
 */

input.new_string:
/**
 * Requests LLM-authored explanations for a set of combinations from the agent's
 * `/explain` endpoint, degrading to deterministic explanations on any failure.
 *
 * The call is bounded by an `AbortController` timeout (`timeoutMs`, or
 * `AGENT_EXPLAIN_TIMEOUT_MS`, default 20s); the timer is always cleared in a
 * `finally` block.
 *
 * Fallback branching (in order):
 * - Non-2xx response, invalid JSON, or a schema that fails `explainResponseSchema`
 *   throws internally and is caught, yielding a deterministic fallback with an
 *   "Agent unavailable" summary.
 * - A valid response whose IDs don't exactly cover the request (see
 *   {@link hasExactExplanationCoverage}) yields a deterministic fallback with an
 *   "omitted or added recommendation IDs" summary.
 * - A valid, fully-covering response that itself sets `fallbackUsed: true` is
 *   replaced with deterministic explanations while preserving the agent's
 *   `analysisSummary`.
 * - Otherwise the agent's response is returned verbatim (`fallbackUsed: false`).
 *
 * This function never rejects: every failure mode resolves to a valid
 * `ExplainResponse`.
 *
 * @param request - The combinations, stats, lucky numbers, and target draw to explain.
 * @param opts - Optional `agentUrl` (defaults to `AGENT_URL` then
 *   `http://localhost:8000`) and `timeoutMs` overrides.
 * @returns An `ExplainResponse` — either the agent's explanations or a
 *   deterministic fallback, always covering exactly the requested combinations.
 */
export async function explainWithAgent(
  request: ExplainRequest,
  opts: { agentUrl?: string; timeoutMs?: number } = {},
): Promise<ExplainResponse> {

content.text:
Dependencies aren't installed in this environment (`node_modules` missing), so I can't run the compiler. That's fine here — the changes are purely additive `/** */` doc comments placed directly above existing signatures, with no code touched, so there's no compilation risk.

## Summary

Added comprehensive JSDoc in the concise prose style of `packages/data/src/sync.ts`.

**`apps/api/src/service.ts`** — all 8 exported functions:
- `createRecommendations` — full docs including the **freshness branching** (live sync → `classifyFreshness`; throw → `last-good` with `syncErrorKind`; `skipLiveSync` → `skipped`) and the **fallback branching** (always-`true` deterministic base, downgraded to `false` only by a reachable/valid agent).
- `saveRecommendations`, `getInternalStatistics`, `getInternalLatestDraws`, `generateInternalCandidates`, `validateInternalCandidates`, `runAgentDevPath`, `checkSavedRecommendation` — purpose, params, returns, and `@throws` for each `ZodError` and `status`-tagged error (400/404/409/502), plus the target-draw "don't silently clamp" branch in `checkSavedRecommendation`.

**`apps/api/src/agentClient.ts`**:
- `explainWithAgent` (exported) — timeout/abort behavior plus the ordered **fallback branching** (non-2xx/invalid JSON/bad schema → "unavailable"; ID-coverage mismatch → "omitted or added IDs"; agent-signaled fallback → deterministic with preserved summary; else verbatim) and the never-rejects guarantee.
- The two private helpers `fallbackResponse` and `hasExactExplanationCoverage` that drive that branching, documented for completeness.

Each block documents purpose, `@param`, `@returns`, and `@throws` as requested.

content.text:
Done. Added a short JSDoc note at every throw site documenting each error code:

**`apps/api/src/service.ts`**
- `400` — target draw already drawn/synced (`createRecommendations`)
- `502` — upstream agent returned non-2xx (`runAgentDevPath`)
- `404` — no saved recommendation for `id` (`checkSavedRecommendation`)
- `409` — target draw hasn't happened yet (`checkSavedRecommendation`)
- `404` — resolved draw number has no stored result row (`checkSavedRecommendation`)

**`apps/api/src/agentClient.ts`** (both internal, caught and routed to the deterministic fallback)
- `agent <status>` — non-2xx from `/explain`
- `agent schema invalid` — body failed `explainResponseSchema`

All changes are comment-only, placed immediately above their respective `throw` statements, complementing the `@throws` tags already in the function-level JSDoc.
